accuracy_notes.graduate_route_change: Masters reduced from 24 to 18 months as of Nov 2025

_meta.integration_note: Static KB fields are ground truth for facts that change infrequently (university programs, visa rules, scholarship eligibility). live_metrics fields are fetched from APIs every 6-24 hours and injected at query time. Never hardcode live_metric values in prompts — always read from live_metrics block.

living_cost_monthly_gbp.note: £1,529/month is UKVI minimum from Nov 2025. Budget £1,700-2,200 for realistic living.

london.part_time_work: £12.21/hour minimum wage (April 2026). 20hrs/week during term = ~£400-450/month after tax.

midlands.character: Affordable, large student populations, strong industry links. Good for Engineering, Business, Computer Science.

north_england.character: Best value for money in UK. Strong research universities. Large Indian student communities.

scotland.note: NHS Scotland free prescriptions for all. Good for health sciences.

south_england_southwest.character: Research-focused, beautiful campuses. Good for Sciences, Engineering, Marine, Environmental.

wales.character: Most affordable UK region. Strong research universities. Cardiff growing tech hub.

northern_ireland.character: Cheapest cost of living in UK. Close to Ireland (no visa needed to travel there).

ielts.overview: International English Language Testing System. Most widely accepted.
ielts.scoring: Band 1-9, reported as overall and individual component scores
ielts.preparation_time: 3-6 months from scratch. 1-2 months if base is strong.
ielts.score_improvement_tips: Practice past papers. Cambridge official materials. Speaking practice with a partner. Writing Task 2 templates.

pte_academic.overview: Pearson Test of English. Computer-based, faster results (48 hours).
pte_academic.advantage: Faster results, more test dates, no human examiner bias

toefl_ibt.overview: Test of English as Foreign Language. Preferred by some US-leaning universities. Less popular for UK but accepted.
toefl_ibt.note: Most UK universities prefer IELTS or PTE. Check specific program requirements.

duolingo_english_test.note: Accepted by very few UK universities. Mainly used for US/Canada. Not recommended for UK applications.

post_study_work_visa.name: Graduate Route (Post-Study Work Visa)
post_study_work_visa.duration_masters: 18 months from November 2025 rule change (was 2 years)
post_study_work_visa.eligibility: Complete eligible UK degree from licensed sponsor
post_study_work_visa.work_rights: Work in any job, any employer, any hours. No sponsorship needed.
post_study_work_visa.switch_to_skilled_worker: Can switch to Skilled Worker visa from within UK if employer sponsors
post_study_work_visa.important_note: Graduate Route reduced from 24 to 18 months by UK Immigration White Paper May 2025. PhD still 3 years.
post_study_work_visa.ete_note: EU citizens may rejoin Erasmus+ post UK-EU deal May 2025 — details pending

_voice_agent_tips.cost_conversation: Always state costs in BOTH GBP and INR. Use live_metrics.exchange_rates.gbp_inr for conversion. Round to nearest ₹1,000 for clarity.
_voice_agent_tips.university_recommendation: Ask field first, then budget, then percentage/CGPA. Map to course_comparison_by_field.
_voice_agent_tips.ielts_advice: Ask current score before advising. If no score yet, estimate prep time from ielts_pte_preparation section.
_voice_agent_tips.graduate_route_clarification: Always clarify: UK = 18 months (Nov 2025 change), Ireland = 24 months. Both allow any job without sponsorship.
_voice_agent_tips.london_vs_outside: If budget is under ₹25 lakh/year total, recommend outside London. London adds ₹5-8 lakh/year to living costs.

why_ireland: English-speaking EU member state — pathway to EU work
No post-Brexit complications — Ireland stayed in EU
Home to European HQs of Google, Meta, Apple, Microsoft, Amazon, Pfizer, Roche
2-year stay back option (Third Level Graduate Scheme) after Masters
Lower fees than UK for many courses
Stamp 2 visa allows 20 hours/week work during term, 40 hours/week during summer
Growing Indian community particularly in Dublin

academic_year.note: CAO (Central Applications Office) handles Irish UG applications. PG direct to universities.

stay_back_visa.name: Third Level Graduate Programme
stay_back_visa.duration_masters: 24 months (2 years) — better than UK's 18 months
stay_back_visa.eligibility: Full-time degree of at least 1 year at recognized Irish HEI
stay_back_visa.note: Strong advantage over UK for students wanting EU stay-back

financial_requirement_visa.note: Ireland requires proof of €10,000 per year for living costs (non-refundable 'settlement' evidence)
financial_requirement_visa.bank_statement_requirement: Funds held for no specified days (unlike UK 28-day rule) but generally 3 months minimum statements required

technological_university_dublin.notable: Ireland's largest technological university. New modern campus. Very affordable fees. High acceptance. Good for students with lower profiles. Strong in Engineering, Business, Computing.

maynooth_university.location: Maynooth, County Kildare (30 min from Dublin)
maynooth_university.notable: 30 minutes from Dublin. Safe, peaceful campus town. Lower cost of living than Dublin. Affordable fees. Strong in Social Sciences, Computer Science, Education. Growing research reputation.

atu_atlantic_technological_university.notable: Very affordable Irish university option. Multiple campus locations on the west coast. High acceptance. Good for students with lower profiles. Galway campus is in a beautiful city.

_voice_agent_tips.total_cost_estimate: For a 1-year UK Masters outside London: Year 1 total ≈ tuition + £12,000-16,000 living + £1,741 visa + £3,000-5,000 pre-departure. Convert at live rate.

_voice_agent_tips.loan_route: If student has no funds, suggest education loan route. SBI Global Ed-Vantage is most common. Prodigy Finance for no-collateral.

uk_nov_2025_rates.28_day_critical_detail: Balance must NOT drop below required amount for even a single day in the 28-day period

calculation_formula.step5: Total = minimum bank statement amount required for 28 days

university_halls.description: On-campus, managed by university. Safest and most convenient for first year.
university_halls.catered_option: Some halls offer meal plan at additional cost
university_halls.tip: Apply for university halls immediately on receiving offer. Priority given to first-year and international students. Fills up by May-June.
university_halls.booking_process: Login to student portal → Accommodation portal → Select room type → Pay deposit (usually £200-400)

private_student_accommodation.description: Purpose-Built Student Accommodation (PBSA) from companies like Unite Students, iQ Student Accommodation, Student Roost, Scape.
private_student_accommodation.tip: Book in January-March for September intake. Flexible short-term contracts available.

private_rental_hmo.description: Houses of Multiple Occupation (HMO) — shared house/flat with other students.
private_rental_hmo.considerations: Must pay Council Tax if no full-time student exemption certificate from university. Gas/electricity usually not included.
private_rental_hmo.tip: Join university Facebook/WhatsApp groups for accommodation leads from current students.

homestay.description: Stay with a UK family. Good for language immersion, cultural experience.
homestay.note: Less popular with Indian students. Good for language improvement.

uk.indian_student_tip: Look for areas with Indian grocery stores and restaurants — Southall/Wembley (London), Rusholme (Manchester), Sparkhill (Birmingham), Leith Walk (Edinburgh). Hugely reduces food costs and homesickness.

university_halls.note: Very limited in Ireland. Severe student accommodation shortage particularly in Dublin.
university_halls.tip: Apply on Day 1 of receiving offer. Dublin accommodation crisis — plan backup options.

private_rental.note: Extreme shortage in Dublin. Book from India if possible. Join Dublin student Facebook groups immediately.

ireland.accommodation_crisis_note: Ireland, especially Dublin, has a severe housing shortage. Many students struggle to find accommodation. Plan 6 months in advance. Consider Cork/Galway/Limerick for more availability.

sop_guidance._description: Used for the on-call SOP Generator feature. Each university has specific prompts.

structure_standard.paragraph_1: Academic background + key achievements + what sparked interest in this specific field
structure_standard.paragraph_2: Professional experience + projects + skills directly relevant to the program
structure_standard.paragraph_3: Why this specific program at this specific university (name specific modules, faculty, research groups)
structure_standard.paragraph_4: Career goals post-graduation and how this degree bridges your current position to your target role

university_specific_prompts.edinburgh_msc_ai: Focus on research motivation. Name specific AI labs at Edinburgh (ILCC, LFCS). Mention specific faculty whose work aligns with your interests. Edinburgh values academic depth over industry experience for this program.
university_specific_prompts.warwick_wbs_mba: Leadership narrative. Business impact examples with numbers. Why Warwick's specific MBA structure (tri-semester, live business projects). Career pivot story if applicable.
university_specific_prompts.ucl_msc_cs: Technical projects with measurable outcomes. Research interests. UCL's interdisciplinary nature. London tech ecosystem aspiration.
university_specific_prompts.manchester_msc_data_science: Data science portfolio. Industry relevance. Manchester's industry partnerships. North England cost advantage (shows financial planning).
university_specific_prompts.imperial_msc_computing: STEM excellence. Technical innovation. Name Imperial's specific research centres. High bar — expect to highlight exceptional academic achievement.
university_specific_prompts.lse_msc_finance: Finance career path. Quantitative skills. LSE's social science foundation to finance. Clear career trajectory.
university_specific_prompts.tcd_ireland_msc_cs: Ireland's tech ecosystem (Dublin = Silicon Docks). EU stay-back advantage if relevant. Research opportunities at TCD.

common_mistakes: Starting with 'Since childhood I have been fascinated...' — cliche, avoid
Repeating CV points without adding context or insight
Generic statements about university ('prestigious institution') without specifics
Not explaining why THIS program at THIS university (not just the subject)

uk_september_2026_intake.may_june_2026: Most programs will have rolling decisions. Some competitive ones may be full.

ireland_september_2026_intake.processing_time: 4-8 weeks for university decision + 6-8 weeks for Irish visa
ireland_september_2026_intake.tight_timeline_note: For September 2026 start: visa application must be submitted by July 2026 at latest.

january_2027_intake_uk.limited_programs: Only ~30% of UK programs offer January intake
january_2027_intake_uk.popular_programs: Coventry, Birmingham City, Northumbria, UWE Bristol, some post-92 universities

3_year_degree_accepted.question: Is my 3-year Indian degree accepted for UK Masters?
3_year_degree_accepted.answer: Yes, most UK universities accept 3-year Indian bachelor's degrees for postgraduate admission. This includes Russell Group universities. Oxford, Cambridge, and a small number of programs may require 4-year degrees or additional qualifications. Always verify on the specific program's entry requirements page. Ireland also accepts 3-year Indian degrees for most Masters programs.

backlog_impact.question: Will backlogs affect my UK/Ireland application?
backlog_impact.answer: 1-2 backlogs, if cleared, are generally not an automatic rejection for post-92 UK universities and Irish universities. Russell Group universities are stricter — any backlogs can disadvantage your application, but it depends on whether they're cleared and your overall profile. Always disclose backlogs honestly. SOP can address them. Edinburgh, UCL, Imperial, LSE tend to reject profiles with backlogs.

gap_year_policy.question: I have a gap year / career gap. Will it affect my application?
gap_year_policy.answer: A gap of under 2 years is generally accepted and even welcomed if explained well. Work experience during gap: Very positive — strengthens application. Gap for exam prep (IELTS, GATE, CAT): Fully acceptable. Family circumstances: Acceptable with brief explanation. Career change gap: Fine if SOP explains learning/upskilling during the period. Problematic: Unexplained gaps of 3+ years with no activity. Key: Address it proactively in your SOP with what you learned/achieved during the gap. Immigration officers look for genuine academic intent — be prepared to explain in visa interview/GSA.

work_rights_during_study.question: Can I work while studying in UK/Ireland?
work_rights_during_study.answer: UK: 20 hours per week during term, full-time during vacations. Minimum wage £12.21/hour (April 2026). Monthly earnings approx £400-450 part-time. Ireland: 20 hours per week during term, 40 hours during holidays (June 15-Sep 15, Dec 15-Jan 15). Minimum wage €13.50/hour.

ielts_waiver.question: Can I get IELTS waiver for UK universities?
ielts_waiver.answer: Some UK universities offer IELTS waiver if: (1) your bachelor's degree was taught entirely in English (provide Medium of Instruction certificate from university), or (2) you're from an English-speaking country (native speakers). The MOI certificate route is the most common way Indian students get a waiver. Not all universities accept this — check specific program requirements.

graduate_route_visa_change.question: Is it still worth going to UK after Graduate Route cut to 18 months?
graduate_route_visa_change.answer: Yes. Even at 18 months, the Graduate Route lets you work in any job at any salary level without needing employer sponsorship. During those 18 months you can switch to a Skilled Worker visa if an employer sponsors you. Ireland's 2-year stay-back is now more attractive by comparison, but UK remains the more established destination with better university brand value for most courses.

cost_comparison_uk_vs_ireland.question: Is UK or Ireland cheaper?
cost_comparison_uk_vs_ireland.answer: Ireland can be cheaper for some programs (lower tuition at UCC, UL, DCU vs London). But Dublin living costs now rival London in expense. Scotland and North England remain the most affordable UK options. Ireland's 2-year post-study work visa gives better value on stay-back. Rule of thumb: for tech and business, Dublin costs roughly the same as London. For research-focused Masters, Scotland or Northern Ireland are cheapest overall.

total_cost_first_year_uk_outside_london.question: What is the total cost for the first year in UK outside London?
total_cost_first_year_uk_outside_london.answer: Total first year cost outside London = Tuition fee + £10,539 living (9 months UKVI rate) + £1,741 visa costs (visa £558 + IHS £1,164 + biometrics £19) + £150,000–200,000 INR pre-departure (IELTS, documents, flights, forex). Example: University of Manchester MSc Data Science = £31,500 tuition + £10,539 living + £1,741 visa = ~£43,780 (≈ ₹47 lakh) in UK spend, plus ~₹2.5 lakh pre-departure India costs. Total: approximately ₹49–50 lakh first year.

total_cost_first_year_uk_london.question: What is the total cost for the first year in London UK?
total_cost_first_year_uk_london.answer: Total first year cost in London = Tuition fee + £13,761 living (9 months UKVI rate for London) + £1,741 visa costs + ₹2–3 lakh pre-departure India costs. Example: UCL MSc Data Science = £38,700 tuition + £13,761 living + £1,741 visa = ~£54,202 (≈ ₹58 lakh) in UK spend, plus ~₹2.5 lakh India costs. Total: approximately ₹60–62 lakh first year for top London universities.

total_cost_first_year_ireland.question: What is the total cost for the first year in Ireland?
total_cost_first_year_ireland.answer: Total first year Ireland = Tuition fee + €12,000–15,000 living (Dublin, ~€1,000–1,250/month x 12 months) or €9,600–12,000 (outside Dublin) + €360 visa + €300 GNIB registration + ₹1.5–2.5 lakh pre-departure. Example: UCD MSc Computer Science = €20,000 tuition + €14,400 living (Dublin) + €660 visa/GNIB = €35,060 (≈ ₹32 lakh) + ₹2 lakh India costs = approximately ₹34–36 lakh first year. Ireland is typically ₹10–15 lakh cheaper than equivalent UK program.

living_cost_breakdown_uk.question: What are the monthly living costs in the UK?
living_cost_breakdown_uk.answer: Monthly living costs UK: Accommodation (shared): London £700-1000, Outside London £400-600. Food: £150-250. Transport: London £100-150 (Oyster Card), Outside London £50-80. Phone: £15-25. Internet: included in most halls. Entertainment/misc: £50-100. Total London: £1,100-1,600/month. Total Outside London: £800-1,200/month. UKVI official proof of funds rate (April 2026): London £1,529/month, Outside London £1,171/month. Note: UKVI rate is minimum bank balance proof, actual spending may be lower.

living_cost_breakdown_ireland.question: What are the monthly living costs in Ireland?
living_cost_breakdown_ireland.answer: Monthly living costs Ireland: Accommodation (shared in Dublin): €700-1,100. Food: €150-250. Transport (Dublin Bus/Leap Card): €80-120. Phone: €15-25. Entertainment/misc: €50-100. Total Dublin: €1,100-1,600/month. Total outside Dublin: €800-1,200/month. Ireland accommodation is scarce — book very early. Students can use leap card for 50% discounted public transport.

visa_bank_balance_requirement.question: How much money do I need to show in my bank for UK student visa?
visa_bank_balance_requirement.answer: UK Student Visa bank balance requirement (Nov 2025 update): If studying in London: £1,529 x number of months in UK (max 9) = up to £13,761. If studying outside London: £1,171 x number of months (max 9) = up to £10,539. PLUS full tuition fee if not already paid. Funds must be held continuously for 28 consecutive days before application date. The 28-day period must END no earlier than 31 days before the application date. Accepted: personal savings, parent's account, Fixed Deposit, loan sanction letter (from select banks). Currency must be converted at current UKVI rate.

application_fee_all_universities.question: What is the application fee for UK and Ireland universities?
application_fee_all_universities.answer: UK Universities: Almost all UK universities charge ZERO application fee for postgraduate (Masters) direct applications. The only fee is if applying through UCAS for undergraduate: £28.50 for up to 5 choices. Ireland Universities: TCD €55, UCD €55, UCC €50, University of Galway €50, University of Limerick varies, DCU FREE, Griffith College FREE, TU Dublin FREE. Note: Some universities may charge a deposit (£500-£3,000) on accepting an offer — this is deducted from tuition.

deposit_after_offer.question: Do I have to pay a deposit after getting an offer?
deposit_after_offer.answer: Yes, most universities require a tuition deposit to secure your place after receiving a conditional or unconditional offer. Typical deposits: Top Russell Group (UCL, Imperial, LSE): £2,000-3,000. Mid Russell Group (Manchester, Sheffield, Leeds): £1,500-2,500. Post-92 (Coventry, Northumbria): £1,000-2,000. Ireland top (TCD, UCD): €500. Ireland others: €200-300. This deposit is deducted from your total tuition fee — you don't pay it on top.

tuition_fee_payment_schedule.question: How do I pay my tuition fees? Can I pay in instalments?
tuition_fee_payment_schedule.answer: Tuition fee payment options: Most UK universities allow splitting fees into 2-3 instalments (typically start of each term). Some post-92 universities allow up to 4-6 monthly instalments. Payment portal is usually the university's online student system. Methods accepted: Bank transfer (SWIFT), Flywire, Western Union, PayMyTuition — all support INR payments directly. Avoid using forex cards for large amounts — use purpose-built education transfer services. Fateh Education can advise on best remittance routes to save on conversion costs.

ihs_what_is_it.question: What is IHS (Immigration Health Surcharge) and how much is it?
ihs_what_is_it.answer: Immigration Health Surcharge (IHS) gives you access to free NHS healthcare in the UK — like having the National Health Service from Day 1. Student rate: £776 per year (discounted from full £1,035). For a 1-year Masters (17-month visa): £776 + £388 (half year) = £1,164. For a 2-year course: £1,552. Paid upfront during visa application, cannot be paid in installments. Mandatory — cannot opt out. BENEFIT: You get unlimited free GP (doctor) visits, hospital treatment, prescriptions at £9.90/item throughout your study.

can_i_work_part_time_uk.question: Can I work part time in the UK while studying?
can_i_work_part_time_uk.answer: Yes! UK Student Visa allows: 20 hours per week during term time. Unlimited hours during official university vacation (Christmas, Easter, Summer). Minimum wage from April 2026: £12.21 per hour. Expected monthly income (20 hrs/week): approximately £400-450. Over a year: approximately £5,000-6,000 (useful towards living costs). Important: You CANNOT work during thesis submission/exam periods if university policy says so. Cannot work as self-employed or set up a business on Student Visa. Ireland allows same 20 hours during term, 40 hours during summer holidays.

scholarship_chances_indian_student.question: What are my chances of getting a scholarship as an Indian student?
scholarship_chances_indian_student.answer: Scholarship chances depend on profile: Chevening (Fully funded): Very competitive, 2-3% acceptance. Needs 2 years work experience, strong profile, leadership. GREAT Scholarship: Competitive, merit-based, £10,000+. Most universities have their own scholarships ranging £2,000-£10,000. Russell Group scholarships: 10-20% of admitted students get some funding. Post-92 scholarships: More accessible, 30-50% may get partial discounts. Strategy: Apply to 5-7 universities, confirm to the one offering best scholarship. University-specific deadlines matter — apply early (Nov-Jan for Sep intake). Fateh Education helps identify and apply for available scholarships.

ielts_minimum_for_uk.question: What IELTS score do I need for UK universities?
ielts_minimum_for_uk.answer: IELTS requirements by university type: Oxford/Cambridge: 7.5 overall, 7.0 each component. Top Russell Group (UCL, Imperial, LSE, Edinburgh): 7.0 overall, 6.5 each component. General Russell Group (Manchester, Leeds, Sheffield, Bristol): 6.5 overall, 6.0 each component. Post-92 UK (Coventry, Northumbria, Brunel): 6.0 overall, 5.5 each component. Some accessible universities: 5.5 overall. Ireland (TCD, UCD): 6.5 overall, 6.0 each. Ireland others: 6.0 overall, 5.5 each. Note: Writing is the most commonly failed component — aim for 6.0 minimum.

ielts_vs_pte.question: Should I take IELTS or PTE for UK/Ireland university applications?
ielts_vs_pte.answer: Both are widely accepted. IELTS (Academic): Accepted by ALL UK and Ireland universities. Paper-based or computer-based. Results in 13 days. Fee: ~₹17,500. PTE Academic: Accepted by most UK universities and Ireland universities. Computer-based only. Results in 5 business days (faster). Fee: ~₹17,000. Advantage of PTE: Faster results, computer-marked writing (some say easier scoring). Advantage of IELTS: Universally accepted, more familiar. Duolingo English Test: Now accepted by some post-92 universities and Coventry, Middlesex — fee is lowest (~₹4,500) but not universally accepted. Recommendation: If applying to top Russell Group, do IELTS. For post-92 or if you need quick results, PTE is fine.

ielts_waiver_moi_certificate.question: Can I get IELTS waiver with Medium of Instruction certificate?
ielts_waiver_moi_certificate.answer: Yes! If your bachelor's degree was taught entirely in English, many UK universities will waive the IELTS requirement. You need: Official Medium of Instruction (MOI) certificate on letterhead from your university registrar/principal, stating the degree was taught entirely in English. Universities accepting MOI waiver: Most post-92 universities, several Russell Group including Manchester, Leeds, Sheffield, Bristol, Newcastle. Universities that usually DO NOT accept MOI (always need IELTS): UCL, Imperial, LSE, Edinburgh, King's College London. Fateh Education can check if your specific university and program accepts MOI in lieu of IELTS.

how_long_is_ielts_valid.question: How long is IELTS valid for university applications?
how_long_is_ielts_valid.answer: IELTS scores are valid for 2 years from the test date. This applies to both university applications AND UK student visa applications. Example: If you took IELTS in February 2025, it's valid until February 2027 for both university and visa purposes. If your score expires between application and visa stage, you MUST retake. Fateh tip: Plan your IELTS at least 3-4 months before your target application deadline. IELTS coaching at Fateh available — 3 to 6 month programs.

three_year_degree_accepted.question: Is my 3-year Indian degree accepted for UK Masters?
three_year_degree_accepted.answer: Yes — great news. The vast majority of UK universities now officially accept 3-year Indian bachelor's degrees for Masters admission. This includes: All major Russell Group universities including UCL, Imperial, Manchester, Edinburgh, Leeds, Bristol, Sheffield. The only exceptions are some Oxford, Cambridge programs and a handful of specialist programs. All Ireland universities accept 3-year Indian degrees. Always verify on the specific program page or with Fateh Education — program-level exceptions exist even within accepting universities.

backlog_policy.question: I have backlogs. Can I still apply to UK/Ireland?
backlog_policy.answer: Depends on number, clearance, and university tier. ALL cleared backlogs: Post-92 UK and most Irish universities accept 1-4 cleared backlogs. Russell Group: 1-2 cleared backlogs accepted at many (Manchester, Leeds, Sheffield, Newcastle, Glasgow, Cardiff). Strict Russell Group (UCL, Imperial, LSE, Edinburgh): Even 1 backlog can be a rejection. Active/uncleared backlogs: Very difficult for any accredited university — clear before applying. Strategy: If you have 3-5 cleared backlogs, target post-92 UK universities or Irish universities. Write a strong SOP explaining the backlogs with context. Coventry, Northumbria, Middlesex, De Montfort, UEL — good options. Fateh Education specializes in finding right fit for backlog profiles.

cas_letter_what_is_it.question: What is a CAS letter and when will I get it?
cas_letter_what_is_it.answer: CAS (Confirmation of Acceptance for Studies) is a unique reference number issued by your UK university AFTER you have accepted an unconditional offer and paid your deposit. It is MANDATORY for your UK student visa application — you cannot apply for a visa without it. Timeline: You accept offer and pay deposit → University checks your eligibility → CAS issued (typically 4-8 weeks before course start) → You apply for visa using CAS number. CAS contains: Your personal details, course details, tuition fee amount, university UKVI sponsorship number. Ireland equivalent: You get an offer letter + receipt of fees — no CAS system in Ireland.

documents_required_for_application.question: What documents do I need to apply to UK/Ireland universities?
documents_required_for_application.answer: Standard documents for Masters application: 1) Bachelor's degree certificate and all semester marksheets/transcripts. 2) 10th and 12th certificates. 3) Statement of Purpose (SOP) / Personal Statement. 4) 2-3 Letters of Recommendation (LORs) from professors or employers. 5) Updated CV/Resume. 6) IELTS/PTE/TOEFL score card (or MOI certificate for waiver). 7) Passport copy (valid at least 6 months beyond course end). 8) Work experience certificates if applicable. 9) Portfolio (for design/architecture programs). 10) GMAT/GRE if required (mainly for MBA and some Finance programs). Fateh Education helps compile and review all documents.

lor_recommendation_letters.question: Who should write my recommendation letters and how many do I need?
lor_recommendation_letters.answer: Most UK and Ireland Masters programs require 2 letters of recommendation (some ask for 3). Who should write them: Academic LOR: From a professor/lecturer who taught you at your bachelor's or master's level — ideally someone who knows your academic work well. Professional/Work LOR: From a manager or senior colleague if you have work experience. For recent graduates with no work experience: Both 2 from academic professors is acceptable. What to include: Specific examples of your academic ability, research skills, teamwork, leadership, character. Avoid: Generic letters, letters from family friends, letters without specific examples. Tip: Brief your recommenders on the program you're applying to so they can tailor their letter. Fateh Education provides LOR guidance templates.

sop_what_to_write.question: What should I write in my Statement of Purpose (SOP)?
sop_what_to_write.answer: A strong SOP has 4 paragraphs in 500-1,000 words: Paragraph 1 — Academic background: Your degree, key subjects, academic achievements, what sparked your interest in this specific field. Paragraph 2 — Professional experience/projects: Work experience, internships, projects directly relevant to the program — with measurable outcomes. Paragraph 3 — Why this specific program at this specific university: Name specific modules, faculty research, labs, industry connections that drew you here. Be specific — not generic. Paragraph 4 — Career goals: What role/industry you want to work in post-graduation, how this degree specifically enables that, and why UK/Ireland is the best place to pursue it. Fateh Education provides SOP review and guidance services.

uk_student_visa_process.question: How do I apply for a UK student visa? What is the process?
uk_student_visa_process.answer: UK Student Visa (Tier 4) process: Step 1: Receive unconditional offer from university. Step 2: Accept offer and pay deposit. Step 3: Get CAS letter from university (usually 6-8 weeks before start). Step 4: Collect all visa documents (passport, CAS, financial evidence, IELTS, transcripts). Step 5: Complete online visa application at gov.uk. Step 6: Pay visa fee (£558 from April 2026) + IHS (£1,164 for 1-year Masters). Step 7: Book VFS Global appointment in India for biometrics (fingerprints + photo). Step 8: Visa decision in 3-8 weeks (priority service available for extra £500). Step 9: Collect visa vignette sticker in passport. Step 10: On arrival in UK, collect BRP (Biometric Residence Permit) within 10 days.

ireland_visa_process.question: How do I apply for an Ireland student visa?
ireland_visa_process.answer: Ireland Long Stay Study Visa (Type D) process: Step 1: Receive offer letter from Irish university. Step 2: Accept offer and pay tuition fee (or large portion). Step 3: Open Irish online visa application at visas.inis.gov.ie. Step 4: Pay visa fee €60. Step 5: Submit application online + post physical documents to Irish Embassy in Delhi or Mumbai within 30 days. Step 6: Wait 6-8 weeks for visa decision. Step 7: On arrival in Ireland, register with INIS (Garda National Immigration Bureau) within 90 days — pay €300 for registration. Step 8: Receive IRP (Irish Residence Permit) card — your official stay permission. Key documents: University acceptance letter, fee payment receipts, financial statements (€7,000-10,000 minimum in account), IELTS, academic certificates, travel insurance.

uk_visa_financial_proof_rules.question: What exact bank balance do I need for UK student visa? What are the rules?
uk_visa_financial_proof_rules.answer: UK Visa Financial Proof Rules (Updated November 2025): Amount needed = Tuition fee balance (if not paid) PLUS living costs. Living: London: £1,529 x months (max 9) = up to £13,761. Outside London: £1,171 x months (max 9) = up to £10,539. Critical 28-Day Rule: Funds must be held CONTINUOUSLY for 28 consecutive days BEFORE you submit your application. The 28-day window must END no more than 31 days before your visa application date. Example: If you apply July 1, your 28-day window must start by June 3 and end by July 1. Cannot dip below the required amount even for 1 day during those 28 days. Accepted: Personal savings account, parent/guardian's account, fixed deposits (if accessible), education loan sanction letter (SBI, HDFC Credila, Axis accepted).

can_family_show_bank_balance.question: Can my parents show the bank balance for my UK visa?
can_family_show_bank_balance.answer: Yes, absolutely. For UK Student Visa, financial evidence can be in your name OR your parents' names (or other family sponsor). If using parents' account: No formal sponsorship letter required (though an accompanying letter of support helps). The account must show the funds for 28 consecutive days. If using a combination of your account and parents' — both accounts can be combined. If the funds are in a Fixed Deposit: Provide FD certificate showing it can be broken if needed. If using an education loan: Provide loan sanction letter — the sanctioned amount counts towards funds evidence. Fateh Education provides detailed document checklist tailored to your visa application.

graduate_route_visa_18_months.question: Is the UK Graduate Route visa worth it now that it is only 18 months?
graduate_route_visa_18_months.answer: Yes, still valuable. The Graduate Route was reduced from 24 to 18 months for Masters graduates in November 2025. What it still gives you: Full 18 months to work in ANY job at ANY salary without employer sponsorship. Use this time to find a sponsored employer for the Skilled Worker visa. Indian IT professionals: Many get sponsored within 6-12 months in UK tech sector (TCS, Infosys, Wipro, Accenture UK, IBM UK all sponsor). Average UK tech graduate salary: £28,000-45,000. Skilled Worker visa minimum salary: £38,700 general, £26,200 for shortage occupations (including some IT roles). Ireland comparison: Ireland gives 2-year stay-back for Masters, now slightly more attractive. But UK university brands are typically stronger for global career prospects.

can_i_bring_family_to_uk.question: Can I bring my spouse/family to the UK on my student visa?
can_i_bring_family_to_uk.answer: UK rules on dependants for students (post-January 2024 rule change): Government-sponsored students: CAN bring dependants. Students on Student Visa at government-funded universities: Can bring spouse and children if they are studying a full-time course at postgraduate level or above. RESTRICTION: Students enrolled on a standard university Masters program (self-funded, no government scholarship): CANNOT bring dependants as of January 2024. This is a significant change — Indian students self-funding their Masters CANNOT bring spouse/children to UK. Ireland: Dependants CAN join you in Ireland if you are enrolled for 1 year+ full-time Masters. The dependant can also work 40 hours/week in Ireland. This makes Ireland more attractive for married students.

dependant_visa_ireland.question: Can my spouse come with me to Ireland while I study?
dependant_visa_ireland.answer: Yes! Ireland allows dependants of international students enrolled in full-time 1+ year programs to accompany them. Your spouse gets a Stamp 3 permission (can live in Ireland but work is restricted under Stamp 3). Work rights for spouse: In 2022 Ireland updated rules — spouses on Stamp 3 as student dependants may apply for work authorization. Recent updates allow Stamp 3 holders married to non-EU students to apply for work permission. Check the current INIS website for latest Stamp 3 work rights. This is significantly better than UK which prohibits student dependants for self-funded Masters students. Fateh Education advises on family-friendly study destinations.

job_market_uk_for_indians.question: How is the job market in the UK for Indian students after Masters?
job_market_uk_for_indians.answer: UK job market for Indian Masters graduates (2026): Tech sector: Strong demand in London, Manchester, Edinburgh. Roles: Software Engineer, Data Scientist, Business Analyst, Cybersecurity Analyst. Typical starting salaries: £28,000-45,000. Finance sector: London is global finance hub. Roles: Investment banking analyst, financial analyst, risk management. Salaries: £35,000-55,000. Other hot sectors: Healthcare IT, AI/ML, Digital Marketing, Supply Chain. Indian community advantage: Large Indian diaspora in UK tech and consulting. TCS, Infosys, Wipro, Accenture, KPMG, Deloitte in UK all actively hire and sponsor Indian graduates. Skilled Worker visa sponsorship: After Graduate Route, employer must sponsor. Minimum salary: £38,700 (as of April 2024, subject to change). Key challenge: Competition is high in London. Networking is critical — attend career fairs, LinkedIn is essential.

job_market_ireland_for_indians.question: How is the job market in Ireland for Indian students?
job_market_ireland_for_indians.answer: Ireland job market for Indian graduates: Tech companies headquartered in Dublin (EMEA hubs): Google, Meta, LinkedIn, Apple, Microsoft, Salesforce, Amazon AWS, Twitter/X, Adobe, Intel, Dell, Oracle. ALL major US tech companies have European HQ in Dublin — hiring is strong. Average starting salary: €35,000-55,000 for tech roles. Finance & Pharma: Dublin's IFSC (International Financial Services Centre) — major banking and fintech hub. Cork: Apple, Amazon Web Services large operations. Limerick: Dell, pharma sector. Visa: 2-year stay-back for Masters. Critical Information: Ireland taxes are high (40%+ above €40,000) — effective take-home is lower than headline salary suggests. Despite this, living costs are lower than London so net position is often comparable.

skilled_worker_visa_uk_requirements.question: What do I need to switch from Graduate Route to Skilled Worker Visa in UK?
skilled_worker_visa_uk_requirements.answer: To switch from Graduate Route to UK Skilled Worker Visa: 1) Job offer from a UK-licensed sponsor employer. 2) Job must be at RQF Level 3+ (graduate level role). 3) Salary must meet the threshold: General: £38,700/year minimum. Shortage occupation list roles: £26,200 minimum (many IT/engineering/health roles qualify). 4) English language requirement: IELTS 6.0 or B1 level. 5) No gap between Graduate Route and Skilled Worker — you can switch before Graduate Route expires. Cost to employer: Visa sponsor fee + Immigration Skills Charge (~£1,000/year). Cost to you: Skilled Worker visa fee (~£600-1,500 depending on duration). Fateh tip: Start networking and applying for sponsored jobs from Month 6 of Graduate Route, not Month 17.

nri_oci_study_uk.question: I am an NRI or OCI holder — any difference in my application?
nri_oci_study_uk.answer: For university applications: NRI/OCI students apply as international students in UK and Ireland — same process as Indian students. You get no fee concession as a 'UK resident' unless you have ILR (Indefinite Leave to Remain) or settled status. For visa: OCI/NRI with valid Indian passport apply for UK Student Visa normally. OCI is not same as British citizenship — you still need a student visa. NRI with OCI card who has been UK resident for 5+ years with settled status: You may qualify for home fee status (domestic student fees) — significant saving. Check with Fateh Education for your specific situation.

education_loan_india.question: Which bank is best for education loan for UK/Ireland studies?
education_loan_india.answer: Best education loans for UK/Ireland studies (April 2026): SBI Global Ed-Vantage: Up to ₹1.5 crore. Interest: ~10.15-11.15%. 15-year repayment. Most trusted by UKVI. HDFC Credila: Up to ₹75 lakh. No collateral up to ₹40 lakh for top universities. Interest: 11.5-13%. Axis Bank: Up to ₹75 lakh. Interest: 12-14%. Prodigy Finance (international lender): No collateral. Loan in GBP/EUR. Good for top-ranked universities. Higher interest (~12-14%) but disbursed in UK. Union Bank of India: Up to ₹40 lakh. ~10.5-11.5%. Section 80E benefit: Interest on education loan is tax deductible for 8 years. Process time: 15-45 days — start early. Fateh Education partners with select lenders for streamlined loan processing.

currency_exchange_best_way.question: What is the best way to send money to UK or Ireland for fees and living?
currency_exchange_best_way.answer: Best services to transfer money to UK/Ireland (April 2026): Flywire: Purpose-built for education payments. Pay in INR, university receives in GBP/EUR. No bank charges. Most universities accept it. Wise (formerly TransferWise): Best exchange rates for regular transfers. Small fixed fee. Used for living expenses (Wise card). Western Union Business: Good for large transfers. Fee negotiable for large amounts. Banks (SWIFT transfer): SBI, HDFC, ICICI — reliable but SWIFT fees (~₹1,500-2,000) + intermediary bank charges. Avoid: Airport exchange counters (worst rates). Using Forex card loaded in INR (high conversion charges). Tax rules: LRS (Liberalised Remittance Scheme) — up to $250,000/year per person tax-free for education. TCS (Tax Collected at Source) 5% on remittances above ₹7 lakh in a year for education — can be claimed back in ITR.

tcs_on_foreign_remittance.question: What is TCS (Tax Collected at Source) on education remittances?
tcs_on_foreign_remittance.answer: TCS (Tax Collected at Source) on LRS remittances for education: Rate: 5% on amounts above ₹7 lakh per year sent via LRS for education (reduced from 20% for education loans). Example: Sending ₹30 lakh for UK tuition. First ₹7 lakh: No TCS. Remaining ₹23 lakh: 5% TCS = ₹1.15 lakh deducted. Can you get it back? YES. TCS is advance income tax collection — you can claim it back by filing your ITR (Income Tax Return). If your total income is below taxable limit (₹3 lakh), full TCS is refunded. Education loans: If fee is remitted directly from an education loan account, TCS rate may be 0.5% or 0%. Consult your bank. Fateh Education advises on TCS minimization strategies.

police_clearance_certificate.question: Do I need a Police Clearance Certificate (PCC) for UK/Ireland student visa?
police_clearance_certificate.answer: UK Student Visa: Generally NOT required as a standard document. However, some individual programs (healthcare, social work, childcare, law enforcement related) may require a Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS) check AFTER arrival in UK. Ireland Student Visa: Similarly, PCC not usually required for standard academic programs. May be required for programs working with minors or vulnerable populations. For Irish long-stay visa: If you have lived in a country for 6+ months in the past 5 years other than India, you may need PCC from that country too. Fateh Education advises on your specific program requirements.

conditional_vs_unconditional_offer.question: What is the difference between conditional and unconditional offer?
conditional_vs_unconditional_offer.answer: Conditional Offer: University wants to admit you, BUT with conditions you must fulfil. Common conditions: Submit final year marksheets (if in final year), achieve minimum percentage (e.g., 60%+), submit IELTS score of 6.5, provide certified translations of documents. HOW TO CONVERT: Meet the conditions → Submit proof → University converts to unconditional. Unconditional Offer (UO): All your documents are verified, no conditions remaining. You can now pay deposit and get CAS letter. Timeline: Accept conditional offer first → Meet conditions → Receive unconditional → Pay deposit → Get CAS → Apply for visa.

september_vs_january_intake.question: Should I choose September or January intake for UK?
september_vs_january_intake.answer: September/October Intake: Most courses available. Better scholarship availability. More networking opportunities with larger cohort. More time for applications. January/February Intake: Only ~30% of courses available (mainly post-92 universities and some at Coventry, Birmingham City, Northumbria, Middlesex). Good if you missed September deadline. Less competition for places. Note: January intake still gets full Graduate Route visa after graduation. For Russell Group universities: September is almost always the ONLY option. Fateh recommendation: September 2026 — apply NOW (April 2026) for remaining spots. September 2027 — ideal planning horizon is October 2026-January 2027.

ucas_vs_direct_application.question: Do I apply through UCAS or directly to the university?
ucas_vs_direct_application.answer: UCAS is used for Undergraduate (UG) degrees only: 1 application, up to 5 university choices, £28.50 fee. Postgraduate (Masters, MBA, PhD): NEVER through UCAS. Apply DIRECTLY to each university through their individual online portals. Each university has its own application portal — you can find it on their website. Application fee: Almost all UK universities have FREE Masters applications. Multiple applications: You can apply to as many universities as you want simultaneously. Recommended: Apply to 6-8 universities across different tiers (2 reaches, 3-4 targets, 2 safety options). Fateh Education manages multiple applications on your behalf.

what_is_russell_group.question: What is Russell Group and why does it matter?
what_is_russell_group.answer: Russell Group is a prestigious alliance of 24 research-intensive UK universities, similar to the Ivy League in the USA. Members include: UCL, Imperial College London, LSE, King's College London, University of Edinburgh, University of Manchester, University of Warwick, University of Bristol, University of Birmingham, University of Leeds, University of Sheffield, University of Nottingham, University of Glasgow, Cardiff University, Queen Mary University of London, Newcastle University, Durham, Exeter, Lancaster, York, Southampton, Queen's Belfast, Cambridge, Oxford. Why it matters for Indian students: Internationally recognised brand — better employer recognition globally. Higher visa application success rates (UKVI trusts these institutions). Stronger alumni networks. Better research reputation. Slightly more difficult admission (55-70%+ typically required). Tip: Russell Group does NOT automatically mean better ROI — check specific program rankings and graduate employment rates.

post_92_universities_good_or_bad.question: Are post-92 / modern universities good? Are they worth it?
post_92_universities_good_or_bad.answer: Post-92 universities (also called 'modern universities' or 'new universities') are legitimate, government-recognised, fully accredited UK universities. They are perfectly valid for UK Student Visa and Graduate Route eligibility. Where they excel: Industry-focused teaching. Higher acceptance rates. More affordable fees. January intake options. Good for students with lower GPAs or backlogs. Examples: Coventry, Northumbria, Middlesex, Westminster, Kingston, UEL, Brunel, Greenwich. Real talk: Employer recognition is lower than Russell Group for top UK employers. International brand recognition is less strong. However: For roles in India or with Indian IT companies, the UK degree brand matters more than the specific university for most positions. Recommendation: Within post-92, prefer Brunel, Middlesex, Surrey, Essex, Hertfordshire — slightly stronger reputations. Fateh Education matches you to the right tier for your profile and career goals.

minimum_gpa_for_uk_masters.question: What is the minimum GPA or percentage to apply to UK Masters?
minimum_gpa_for_uk_masters.answer: UK Masters percentage requirements vary by university tier: Oxford/Cambridge: 75%+ (Distinction / First Class equivalent). Top Russell Group (UCL, Imperial, LSE): 70%+ (Upper Second Class 2:1 equivalent). General Russell Group (Manchester, Leeds, Edinburgh, Bristol): 60-65% (Lower Second Class 2:2 acceptable at some). Some Russell Group (Cardiff, Newcastle, Glasgow): 55-60% acceptable. Post-92 universities (Coventry, Northumbria, Middlesex): 50-55%. Most accessible (Teesside, Sunderland, Bradford): 40-50%. Ireland: TCD, UCD: 60%+. UCC, Galway: 55%+. DCU, Griffith, TU Dublin, ATU: 50%+ or less. Note: GPA requirements are guidelines — strong SOP, work experience, and LORs can compensate for borderline GPA at many universities.

oxford_cambridge_admission.question: Can Indian students get into Oxford or Cambridge?
oxford_cambridge_admission.answer: Yes, Indian students are admitted to Oxford and Cambridge, but extremely competitive: Requirements: 75%+ marks (First Class equivalent). IELTS: 7.5 overall, 7.0 minimum per component. Strong research background/publications for research programs. For MBA programs: GMAT 700+. For law: Strong analytical writing samples. Oxford Acceptance Rate for Masters: ~15-20% overall, lower for competitive programs. Cambridge Acceptance Rate: ~15-20% for graduate programs. Note: These are world's top 2-5 universities — worth applying with the right profile. Fateh Education has placed students at Oxford and Cambridge. Contact for detailed profile assessment.

student_life_uk.question: What is student life like in the UK for Indian students?
student_life_uk.answer: Student life in UK for Indian students: Indian community: Large, established Indian communities especially in London, Birmingham, Manchester, Leicester. Indian restaurants, temples, grocery stores (Patel Bros, Sikh temples) widely available. Weather: UK is mild and wet. Average 5-15°C in winter, 18-25°C in summer. Rain year-round. Pack warm layers and a good waterproof jacket. University societies: Most universities have an Indian Students Society (ISA) — great for networking and cultural events. Holi, Diwali celebrations common. Festivals: Diwali and Holi celebrated by universities. Indian food: Available everywhere — Indian restaurants in every major city. Cook Indian food in your room — most halls have shared kitchens. Safety: UK is generally safe. Normal urban precautions apply. NHS healthcare: Free with IHS paid. Transport: London has excellent Underground (Tube) and bus. Outside London: trains (use Trainline app), buses, or bicycle. Student railcard (25% off) is highly recommended.

indian_grocery_uk.question: Can I find Indian groceries and food in the UK?
indian_grocery_uk.answer: Yes! Indian groceries are widely available across UK: Supermarkets: Tesco, Sainsbury's, Asda stock Indian basics (rice, lentils, spices, frozen Indian meals). Indian grocery stores: In most cities with Indian communities. London: Southall, East Ham, Wembley have entire shopping streets. Birmingham: Handsworth, Alum Rock. Manchester: Rusholme ('Curry Mile'), Old Trafford. Leicester: Belgrave Road. Edinburgh: Southside. Brands available: Shan, MDH, Mother's Recipe, Maggi, Haldirams, MTR (ready meals). Cost: Indian groceries slightly more expensive than India but comparable to UK supermarkets. Tip: Stock up on key Indian spices in the first week — you'll save money cooking Indian food vs eating out.

sim_card_uk.question: Which SIM card should I use in the UK as an Indian student?
sim_card_uk.answer: Best UK SIM cards for Indian students (2026): giffgaff: Most popular among Indian students. Uses O2 network. No contract. Pay-as-you-go or monthly £10-20. Good data packages. Recommended first choice. O2: Good coverage, student deals. EE: Best rural coverage if going to Scotland/Wales. Three: Best for international calls and data. Vodafone: Extensive coverage. Smarty: Affordable data SIM on Three network. Setup: Buy online and get delivered to your accommodation, or buy at any phone shop/supermarket on arrival. Data: Get at least 10GB minimum — UK university campuses have WiFi but mobile data is needed. International calling to India: Use WhatsApp/FaceTime on WiFi (free). Or use Rebtel/Matrix SIM for cheaper international minutes. Airtel and Jio currently don't have UK operations — you'll need a new UK SIM.

opening_bank_account_uk.question: How do I open a bank account in the UK as an international student?
opening_bank_account_uk.answer: Easiest options for Indian students: Digital banks (FASTEST — open before arrival): Wise (TransferWise): Best for international transfers. Open in India before flying. Get GBP account. No minimum balance. Monzo: Open online, great app, no fees. Get account number within 24 hours. Revolut: Multi-currency card. Good for travel. Traditional banks (need to be in UK first): Lloyds, Barclays, HSBC, NatWest — usually require you to be enrolled and have a UK address. Some require an appointment (6-8 week wait). Tip: Open Wise or Monzo as your main account immediately. It works as a UK bank account for all purposes — receiving salary, paying rent, receiving tuition fee refunds. You do NOT need to wait for a traditional bank account.

nhs_uk_what_is_it.question: What is NHS and how do I use it as a student in the UK?
nhs_uk_what_is_it.answer: NHS (National Health Service) is the UK's free public healthcare system. As a student who has paid IHS, you have FULL access to NHS — same as UK citizens. How to access: Register with a GP (General Practitioner / local doctor) within first 2 weeks of arrival. Find nearest GP at nhs.uk. Registration is FREE. GP services: Doctor consultations (free). Prescriptions (£9.90 per item as of 2026, free in Scotland, Wales, N. Ireland). Referrals to specialists. Emergency: A&E (Accident & Emergency) at hospitals — go directly for emergencies. Call 999 for ambulance (free). Call 111 for non-emergency medical advice (free). Dental: NHS dental care available but often limited — private dental check-up £50-80 if needed. Mental health: UK universities provide free counselling services. NHS IAPT services. Fateh pre-departure tip: Bring 3-6 months supply of any regular prescription medication from India — medicines available in UK but may have different brand names.

travel_within_uk.question: How do I travel within the UK cheaply as a student?
travel_within_uk.answer: Budget travel within UK: Train: 16-25 Railcard (£30/year) gives 1/3 off most train journeys — MUST buy. Book advance tickets on Trainline or thetrainline.com (up to 12 weeks ahead — 50-70% cheaper). Megabus/National Express: Very cheap coach service between cities (£5-20 typical). London Underground: Oyster card or contactless payment. Buy monthly/annual Travelcard for regular commuting. Bus in cities: Bus pass for students — check your local council. Cycling: Many universities offer bike schemes. Cheap, eco-friendly for short distances. Flights within UK: Budget airlines (Ryanair, easyJet) for longer distances like London-Edinburgh (~£20-50 booked early). Eurostar: London to Paris in 2.5 hours. Student fares available. First year tip: Get your 16-25 Railcard and TOTUM (Student Discount) card immediately.

fateh_training_services.note: Fateh Education provides IELTS and PTE coaching. Courses available.

ielts_individual_component_guidance.listening: Band 6.5-7.5. Practice with Section 3-4 (academic) recordings. BBC podcasts.
ielts_individual_component_guidance.reading: Band 6.5-7.5. True/False/Not Given, matching headings. Timing critical — 60 min for 40 questions.
ielts_individual_component_guidance.writing: Most challenging. Task 1 (graph/chart) + Task 2 (essay). Consistent vocab and grammar. 250 words Task 1, 250+ Task 2.
ielts_individual_component_guidance.speaking: Band 6.5-7.0. Part 1 casual, Part 2 cue card, Part 3 discussion. Practice with examiner-style partner.

genuine_student_assessment._description: Guidance for the Genuine Student Test (formerly GTV) — immigration officers assess if student is genuinely studying
genuine_student_assessment.what_is_gst: UK UKVI assesses whether visa applicant is a genuine student. Ireland INIS does similar check.
key_questions_officers_may_ask: Why did you choose this specific university?
Why did you choose this specific course?
What are your career plans after graduating?
How will this degree help you in your home country?
Have you researched the university and course content?
What modules does your course cover?
How are you funding your studies?
Do you have any family in the UK/Ireland?
genuine_student_assessment.advice: Students should be able to clearly articulate why they chose the specific university and course. Generic answers like 'it's a good university' are red flags. Know 3-4 specific modules, a professor's name, and a genuine career reason.
genuine_student_assessment.low_risk_nationalities: Some nationalities are exempt from full GST evidence check in UK. Check gov.uk list. India is NOT on the low-risk list — full documentation expected.
genuine_student_assessment.fateh_service: Fateh Education provides Genuine Student Assessment (GSA) preparation coaching — mock interviews, documentation review.

quick_reference._note: Exchange rate fields (gbp_inr_approx, eur_inr_approx) are STATIC FALLBACKS only. Always prefer live_metrics.exchange_rates.gbp_inr for any real calculation.

_live_metrics._description: All fields here are refreshed by background agents. Never use stale static values for these. Each field includes its API source, refresh interval, and Redis cache key.

gbp_inr.note: 1 GBP = approx INR 107.5 (April 2026). Fluctuates ±3-5 INR monthly.

exchange_rates.counsellor_tip: Always convert GBP figures to INR using live rate when explaining costs to Indian students. £1 = ₹107-108 as of April 2026 but confirm from live_metrics.exchange_rates.gbp_inr before quoting.

ihs_per_year_gbp.note: Students pay discounted rate of £776/year. Dependants pay full £1,035/year.

flight_prices._note: These are indicative ranges updated daily from Skyscanner/Google Flights API. Use for counselling estimates only. Actual fares vary by date, airline, and booking window.

bom_dub.direct_flights: Air India operates Mumbai-Dublin direct occasionally. Usually via transit.

baggage_tips.standard_allowance_kg: 23-30kg checked depending on airline. Air India most generous (2x23kg).
baggage_tips.tip: Ship heavy winter clothes, books, utensils by sea courier. Carry essentials + 1 month clothes in flight.

_agent_routing._description: Maps student question intents to KB sections and response strategies. The voice agent uses this to retrieve the right KB section for each turn.

cost_inquiry.response_strategy: Always give INR equivalent. Break down into categories: tuition + living + visa + pre-departure. Give total first year estimate.

university_inquiry.response_strategy: Match to student profile. Mention: ranking, course fees, IELTS requirement, acceptance rate, location, notable fact.

visa_inquiry.response_strategy: Give exact current fee. Explain IHS. Calculate 28-day bank balance requirement if university is known.

ielts_pte_inquiry.response_strategy: Ask if they have a score already. If not, advise prep time and Fateh coaching. Match their target university's requirement.

scholarship_inquiry.response_strategy: Start with Chevening if eligible (check 2yr work exp). Then university-specific. Always mention GREAT scholarship for Masters.

post_study_work.response_strategy: UK = 18 months Graduate Route (any job, no sponsor). Ireland = 2 years. Mention Skilled Worker visa switch possibility for UK.

course_inquiry.response_strategy: Match to student's background. CS/IT → MSc CS, Data Science, AI. Commerce → MBA, MSc Finance. Engineering → MEng, MSc Advanced Engineering.

ireland_inquiry.response_strategy: Highlight 2yr stay-back advantage. Mention EU tech companies. Compare cost to UK. Note Dublin is expensive like London.

document_inquiry.response_strategy: Give category-wise checklist. Emphasize SOP importance. Ask about their specific situation.

loan_inquiry.response_strategy: SBI Global Ed-Vantage first (lowest interest). Mention 80E tax benefit. Prodigy Finance if no collateral. Give realistic EMI estimate.

general_profile_collection.response_strategy: Collect naturally. Don't ask more than 2 questions per turn. Acknowledge each answer warmly before asking next.

hot_signals: Has funding ready or loan sanctioned

course_comparison_by_field._voice_agent_tip: When student mentions their field, use this section to quickly give 3-4 options across budget ranges.

engineering.note: UK has strong engineering programs. MEng is 4-year UG; MSc is 1-year PG (for students with engineering bachelor's).

voice_agent_scripts._description: Ready-to-use conversation flows and response templates for the AI voice agent. Use these verbatim or adapt them based on conversation context.

opening_greet.english: Hello! Welcome to Fateh Education, where we've helped over 45,000 students fulfil their dream of studying abroad. My name is Aria, and I'm here to help you explore your options for studying in the UK or Ireland. May I know your name?
opening_greet.hindi: Namaste! Fateh Education mein aapka swagat hai, jahan humne 45,000 se adhik students ki study abroad ki sapne poori karne mein madad ki hai. Main Aria hoon, aur aaj main aapki UK ya Ireland mein padhne ki opportunities explore karne mein help karunga. Kya main aapka naam jan sakta hoon?
opening_greet.marathi: Namaskar! Fateh Education madhe aapale swagat ahe, jethe amhi 45,000 peksha jast vidyarthyanna UK ani Ireland madhye shiknyasaathi madad keli aahe. Mi Aria aahe. Aapale naav sangal ka?

voice_agent_scripts.response_to_uk_cost_question: Great question about costs! For UK universities outside London, your total first-year spend is typically between 35 to 55 lakh rupees. This breaks down as: tuition fee ranging from 18 lakh for post-92 universities up to 42 lakh for top Russell Group, plus about 11 lakh for living expenses over 9 months, plus roughly 1.87 lakh for your visa and health surcharge. London universities cost 10 to 15 lakh more due to higher living costs. Would you like me to give you a more precise estimate based on your specific target universities and budget?
voice_agent_scripts.response_to_ireland_cost_question: Ireland is a fantastic, slightly more affordable option compared to the UK! Your total first-year cost in Ireland is typically between 28 to 40 lakh rupees. Tuition at top Irish universities like Trinity College Dublin and UCD ranges from 17 to 22 thousand euros. Living costs in Dublin are roughly 1,100 to 1,600 euros per month. The visa fee is very low — just 60 euros. And the stay-back period after Masters is 2 full years! Would you like to compare specific UK vs Ireland options for your profile?
voice_agent_scripts.response_to_ielts_question: For UK universities, IELTS requirements depend on the university tier. For top Russell Group universities like UCL, Imperial or LSE, you need 7.0 overall with minimum 6.5 in each component. For mid-tier Russell Group like Manchester, Leeds or Edinburgh, 6.5 overall with 6.0 minimum. For post-92 universities like Coventry or Northumbria, 6.0 overall with 5.5 minimum. The most challenging section is usually Writing — I'd recommend focusing extra practice there. Does Fateh Education offer IELTS coaching? Yes, we do! We have 3 and 6 month programs. Would you like more details?
voice_agent_scripts.response_to_backlog_question: Having backlogs doesn't automatically close all doors — it really depends on the number and whether they're cleared. If you have 1 to 2 cleared backlogs, many good universities will still consider you, including some Russell Group ones like Manchester, Leeds, Sheffield and Newcastle. Post-92 universities like Coventry, Northumbria, Middlesex — they are quite accessible even with 3 to 5 cleared backlogs. The key is writing a strong Statement of Purpose that addresses the situation positively. Uncleared backlogs are harder — we'd want those cleared before applying. Can you tell me how many backlogs you have and if they're cleared?
voice_agent_scripts.response_to_graduate_route_question: The UK Graduate Route visa is still very valuable, even after it was reduced from 24 to 18 months in November 2025. You get 18 months to work in literally any job, at any salary, without needing employer sponsorship. This gives you time to find the right company that will sponsor your Skilled Worker visa. In the UK tech sector specifically, companies like TCS UK, Infosys UK, Wipro, Accenture, and IBM UK regularly sponsor Indian graduates. So the path is: Graduate Route for 18 months, find sponsor, switch to Skilled Worker. Ireland gives 2 years stay-back if that timeline concerns you.
voice_agent_scripts.response_to_family_question: This is an important consideration. The rules changed in January 2024 for UK. If you are self-funding your Masters on a UK Student Visa, unfortunately you cannot currently bring your spouse or children with you. However, if you have a government scholarship like Chevening, dependants are allowed. Ireland is much more family-friendly — your spouse CAN join you in Ireland and recent rule changes allow them to apply for work permission too. So for married students especially, Ireland can be a significantly better option. Would you like to explore Irish university options?
voice_agent_scripts.response_to_scholarship_question: There are several scholarship options for Indian students! The most prestigious is Chevening — fully funded by the UK government, covering tuition, living, flights, and visa. But it requires 2 years of work experience and is quite competitive. The GREAT Scholarship offers £10,000+ at various UK universities. Scotland offers the Saltire Scholarship worth £8,000 specifically for Indian citizens. Most universities also have their own scholarships ranging from £2,000 to £10,000 — we help identify and apply for these as part of our service. What's your target country and program? I can tell you which specific scholarships you might be eligible for.
voice_agent_scripts.fallback_will_connect_counsellor: That's an excellent and detailed question — let me ensure you get the most accurate and personalised answer. I'm going to flag your query for one of our expert counsellors who will call you back within 2 hours. They can walk you through all the specifics for your exact profile. Is the best number to reach you the one you called from?
voice_agent_scripts.lead_qualification_closing: Thank you so much for all this information! Based on what you've shared, it sounds like you're a strong candidate for study in {target_country}. I'm going to have one of our senior counsellors from Fateh Education reach out to you within {timeframe} to discuss your specific shortlist and next steps. They'll also share some scholarship opportunities that fit your profile. Before I let you go — is there anything specific you'd want to make sure they address in that follow-up call?
voice_agent_scripts.sentiment_detection_confused: I can sense you might have a lot of questions and it can feel overwhelming — that's completely normal! Study abroad is a big decision with lots of moving parts. Let me simplify things. The most important first step is usually getting your IELTS score, then shortlisting universities based on your budget. Everything else — visa, documents, accommodation — comes after. Shall we start with understanding your academic background and what field you want to study?
voice_agent_scripts.sentiment_detection_hesitant: It sounds like you might have some concerns or uncertainties. That's completely understandable — this is a major life decision! Many of our 45,000+ successful students had similar doubts before they made the move. Sometimes the biggest obstacle is just knowing where to start. What's the main thing that's making you hesitant right now? Is it the cost, the visa, leaving home, or something else? I'd love to address your specific concern.
